steps.title: Clear fractions
steps.content: Multiply both sides of $\frac{1}{cx}=\frac{x}{72}+\frac{1}{c}$ by $72cx$: $$72=cx^2+72x.$$ So the equation becomes $$cx^2+72x-72=0.$$

steps.title: Solve the discriminant equation
steps.content: A quadratic has exactly one real solution when its discriminant is $0$. Here the discriminant is $$72^2-4(c)(-72)=72^2+4c(72).$$ Set the discriminant equal to $0$ and solve to get $c=-18$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Solving by hand gives $$72^2+288c=0$$ $$5{,}184+288c=0$$ $$c=-18.$$ The answer is -18.
